title: Lesson 43 — Tangled Up

words.example: An intricate clockwork mechanism filled the case.

words.definition: excessively complicated, typically involving hidden or devious workings
words.example: The tax code's byzantine rules defeat most accountants.

words.definition: extremely complex and difficult to follow
words.example: His convoluted explanation only deepened the confusion.

words.definition: intricate and confusing, like a maze
words.example: The old city's labyrinthine alleys defeated every map.

words.definition: consisting of many interconnected parts; complicated
words.example: A complex system with dozens of moving parts.

words.definition: treating complex issues as simpler than they really are
words.example: The proposal offered a simplistic fix for a deep problem.

words.definition: relating to the basic elements of a subject; simple
words.example: An elementary mistake that any beginner might make.

words.definition: involving only the most basic principles; undeveloped
words.example: He had only a rudimentary grasp of the language.

words.definition: uncomplicated and easy to do or understand
words.example: The assembly instructions were refreshingly straightforward.

words.definition: not difficult to understand or deal with; simple
words.example: An uncomplicated recipe with only four ingredients.

words.definition: to cause to become twisted together with something; to involve in complications
words.example: The fishing line entangled itself around the propeller.

words.definition: to investigate and solve or explain something complex; to undo threads
words.example: It took a year to unravel the fraud's full extent.

story: The merger agreement was famously [[intricate|intricate]] — critics called it [[byzantine|byzantine]], so [[convoluted|convoluted]] that even the lawyers got lost in its [[labyrinthine|labyrinthine]] clauses. It was, everyone agreed, a genuinely [[complex|complex]] document, and any [[simplistic|simplistic]] summary would miss the danger buried inside. Compare that to the founders' original handshake deal: [[elementary|elementary]], almost [[rudimentary|rudimentary]], a [[straightforward|straightforward]] and [[uncomplicated|uncomplicated]] promise on a single page. Somewhere between the two, years of lawyers had managed to [[entangle|entangle]] a simple idea in red tape it would take a new team months to [[unravel|unravel]].
